title: Developer vows to present more ‘options’ for Davis Square tower project amid neighborhood opposition
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Copper Mill developers plan to present more project options for the 26-story Davis Square tower after community opposition.
The proposed development includes 502 apartment units, with 126 units designated as affordable housing in Somerville.
Copper Mill will open a project office on March 23 to provide information and gather community input on the project.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for his 2 nephews with down syndrome
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Steven Ponte, 37, from Somerville, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor his nephews with Down syndrome.
Ponte's twin nephews, Sebastian and Zachary, were diagnosed with Down syndrome shortly after birth, inspiring his marathon run.
The Massachusetts Down Syndrome Congress supported Ponte's family during his nephews' hospitalizations, motivating him to run the marathon.

title: Boston firefighter, National Guard member reportedly injured in strike that killed 6 U.S. soldiers
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston firefighter and Army National Guard Major Mark Garcia was injured in an Iranian drone strike in Kuwait.
The drone strike resulted in the deaths of six U.S. soldiers stationed in Kuwait, where Garcia was also present.
Garcia is currently recovering in Germany, with plans for his return to be celebrated in a few months.

title: Lowell murder trial delayed after lead State Police investigator allegedly causes fatal crash while driving drunk
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Lowell murder trial delayed due to police investigator involved in a fatal crash while allegedly driving drunk.
Sgt. Scott Quigley investigated the case; a December 2023 crash he was involved in led to a man's death.
Justice Barry-Smith cites investigation into Quigley as reason for trial postponement; no new trial date set.

title: ​Bruins’ playoff hopes might rest on Jeremy Swayman’s shoulders for this stretch run
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jeremy Swayman posted a .906 save percentage through 42 games, showing improvement from last season.
Swayman achieved a 9-0 record with a .939 save percentage during Boston's 13-game home win streak.
Bruins are in second Wild Card spot in the East, with 78 points, relying on Swayman for final 18 games.

title: Watch: Body cam shows Patriots party at downtown Boston restaurant
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Boston police released body cam footage from a private Patriots' party at Estella restaurant, showing hookahs and strippers present.
Owner Helder George Brandao faced violations for smoking, unauthorized entertainment, and after-hours drinking; no arrests were made.
The Boston Licensing Board imposed a three-day license suspension on Estella, with two days held in abeyance for one year.

title: 4 things to know about new Patriots safety Kevin Byard
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots sign safety Kevin Byard to a one-year, $9 million deal after his All-Pro season with the Bears.
Byard led the NFL with seven interceptions in 2025, contributing to the Bears' top turnover differential.
Byard has not missed a game in his 10-year career, playing despite significant injuries.

title: Man resentenced in 2011 slaying of Dorchester teen
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Nyasani Watt was resentenced to 14-17 years in prison for the 2011 shooting of Dorchester teen Jaivon Blake.
Watt's initial conviction was overturned in 2024 due to denial of right to counsel, making him eligible for parole now.
Watt pleaded guilty to manslaughter and related charges; co-defendant Sheldon Mattis also convicted, seeking retrial.

title: Samuel Adams launches new ‘Our City. Our Beer.’ variety pack inspired by Boston sports stars
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Samuel Adams launches 'Our City. Our Beer.' variety pack featuring beers inspired by four Boston sports athletes.
The pack includes beers themed around Derrick White, Garrett Crochet, Julian Edelman, and Zdeno Chara.
A public launch party will be held at Banners Kitchen & Tap in Boston on April 4, featuring all four athletes.

title: Boston Marathon race director to run with his 3 kids — and astronaut Sunita Williams
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Dave McGillivray will run his 54th Boston Marathon with his children and astronaut Sunita Williams.
McGillivray began his marathon journey in 1972 and has completed the race every year since 1973.
The 2026 run will honor Susan Hurley, with McGillivray's children raising funds for various causes.
